publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-05-25 00:13:46 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of documents accumulated while Harrisson worked as Curator of the Sarawak Museum, including maps, photographs, notebooks and papers/manuscripts by Harrisson and research assistants.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Tom Harrisson Central Borneo Documents, #8623. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Tom Harrisson was a British polymath. In the course of his life he was an ornithologist, explorer, journalist, broadcaster, soldier, guerrilla, ethnologist, museum curator, archaeologist, documentarian, film-maker, conservationist and writer. After serving in WWII, he became the Curator of the Sarawak Museum from 1947-1966.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The journals are titled with the initals of Tom Harrison, the year the journal was created, and the the number of the journal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bangsa Apau Kayan. 2 p. Census of the Apau Kayan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manis Manuscript. The original handwritten manuscript is in Malay, in five schoolbooks. The English translation follows the page numbers of the Malay manuscript. The translator is W.U. The first page indicated that the information was gathered in January 1950; for some reason, the documents, initially numbered 50/1 to 5, were renumbered 48/1 to 5. Notes indicated that the informations for vol. 1 and 2 were Lenjau and Jok Used, for vols. 3 and 5, A ban U an Tingang of Long Tikan (died 1954).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The origin of the Apau Kayan inhabitants. The origin of the faith in Bungan Malan. About menyat tanah. Old faith bird and others. No longer believe now.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Continuation: the doctrine of Jok Apui. The affairs in the village at the present day. About Lenjau Used and his companions who came over to Sarawak.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kenyah verse; Balan Nyaring story. Uyau Ambing Lian died due to sulkiness. The first coming of proper and orderly government to Sarawak. First coming of government into the Baram. About the intention of Pangeran Muda Susu to invade Berunai

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [Cont.] Tuan Aut and Pangulu Tama Bulan The Kalabits were subdued. The Ken yah of the upper Rejang run away. Batang Kayan Kenyah made a war in revenge to those in the Selaan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [Cont.] The death of Tam a Bulan and the Berunai people's decision to shift the Kenyahs. (The Kenyah run away to the Kayan river). (The people of Brunei attack the Baram; they are driven away by the Rajah). 20 N.B. The titles between brackets are in the Malay text, but not the translation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Population of Long Buroi Sebop. 6 p., handwritten .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH, Penghulu Apoi Injau of Long Kemuat, Bahau river (revised work for research assistant) , 1 p .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH with Penghulu Apoi Injou. Handwritten exercise book, 31 p. Almost illegible.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Bahau under Penghulu Apoi Injou by Tom Harrisson. Schoolbook, handwritten by TH, 10 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. Meaning of term "Bahau" and all its uses 2. Present tribal (group) organisation of Indonesian Kenyahs 3. Relation of Indonesian to Sarawak Kenyah 4. Relations of Kenyah and Kayan 5. Relation between Kenyah and Saban of Ulu Bahau 6. Relation of Kenyahs and Badangs 7. Origin stories and myths of Kenyahs 8. Full story of Kenyah migration 9. The Kenyah groups and marriage laws 10. Punan stories and relations with the Kenyahs 11 . Stones and Kudis 12. Mr. Harrisson's notes on Kenyah class structure [In manuscript form , typed by J .R.] 13. Funeral rites (by class) 14. Genealogies, by Temenggong Lawai Jau, M.B.E., Penghulu Liim Bilong and Penghulu Apoi Injou

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ken Exercise books 2/58 - 14/58, 10/58 missing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. Origin stories and myths of the Kenyah Transcribed by J.R., 1 p. 2. Full accounts of Kenyah migrations. 6 p . 3. Relation of Indonesia to Sarawak Kenyah , 1 p. Transcribed by J .R. 4. How the Kenyah got split into groups and what these group terms mean and how they work standard marriage laws and funeral rites by class. 18 p. 5. Relations of Kenyahs and Kayans and how they originated differently , etc., 3 p. Transcribed by J .R. 6. Relations of Kenyah and Sabans (Ulu Bahau) , 1 p. 7. Relations of Kenyahs and Badangs by Pa Bit Enjok . 3 p. 8. Relations of Kenyahs and Punans. Not available ; see note above. 9. Present distribution and numbers of Indonesian Punans. Not available ; see note above . 10. All known Ken yah stories especially about historical stones and kudi ; L. Balau (cave burial) [by TH] ; Gan Noyang [by TH]; Ancient food of human being; Ad at Lamimpa; Adat anak or "Notong pusha" . Only the last two sections have been transcribed; the schoolbook has 31 p. 11. Divisions of movable properties by the Ken yah. 4 p . 12. How are the seasons, years (calendar) worked by Kenyah. 3 p. 13. Who lived before the Kenyahs and Kayans in their respective countries before them . The book contains 3 p. by Sandin and 12 by TH. Transcribed by J .R . 14. Kenyah/Kayan contact with N.E.I. Government. 7 p. 15. How Christianity and Bungan religion come to Kenyah? 6 p . 16. Head hunter war. 6 p . 17. Kenyah/Kayan simple vocabulary. Handwritten . 5 p . plus notes byTH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pa Bit's comments on various exhibits in the Museum. 7 p. (also in handwritten form, 13 p., schoolbook) .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter of Sandin to TH. 2 p . about KEN 1959 series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Temenggong Oyong Lawai Jau to TH. Handwritten original in Malay and translation ; on bark coats .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ken - 10/59 Adat Lamimpa

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mamat. Exercise book in TH's hand. Approximately 50 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three Leppo Tau punishments, by Pa Bit Enjok and TH, 4 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dyak tribe migrates. Copied from Times of Indonesia, vol. 23, no. 45, p. 4, 1 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Belaga revisited 1932-1960. Handwritten by TH. Names of pupils in Abun Matu school, Belaga: than every races [sic], 22 II 60, 1 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: LNK 1961 SERIES The original notebooks are not available (with exceptions noted below) . The documents are numbered LNK/1/61 to LNK/8/61. There is no LNK/7 /61. 'LNK' probably stands for 'Long Nawang Kenyah'. 1. The origin of the Kenyah; the origin of the Mamat feast. The order of dress; the birding cult. 22 p. 2. The Nutong Pusha feast or a feast for naming the child. Includes: the Nutong Pusha feast. The farming rules and regulations. Planting of padi. The weeding season. The harvest. 7 p. 3. The life of the Kenyah from birth to death. Contents: Birth; boyhood; bachelorhood; marriage; sick (Uyan Udip); sick (Dayong); death and burial. 21 p. 4. The building of the new long-house . 6 p. 5. Muko mending (a ceremony held for driving away diseases). 6 p. 6. The Kenyah of Borneo, 5 p. by Pa Uloi Apoi, assisted byPa Poi Anye, 64 years old. 7. [not available] 8. Mamat dress check list. The order of mamat dress.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exercise book "Marp.at dress" 10 p., handwritten by Sandin, with notes by TH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exercise book "the order ofMamat dress, book 2", with 10 pages of drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tena Payau statue by Pa Tuloi Bit, assisted by Pa Poi Anye and Pa Jalong Bilong, with date handwritten by TH, 1/2/61, and note LNK/1/61, although link to LNK series is not clear.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Two Orang stories from Central Borneo. 5 p., handwritten. On Kayan and Punan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Kenyah songs and prayers recorded by B. S[ an din] at Bungaiow annex. Handwritten, 3 p., in schoolbook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH The Hornbill, 2 p., handwritten by TH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Leppo Tau. Handwritten by TH, 23 p. Exercise book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Long Nawang: "Tree of life" ; omens; mamat and hombill; dayong; masks and bark coats; the Pua people: megalithic: ulu Baloi. 11 p. Also manuscript in TH's hand, 14 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation from J.M. Elshout, 1926. "De Kenja-Dajaks uit het Apo-Kajangebied", Chapter III, section VI on Mamat rituals. 88 p. [This may have been translated by Frans Baartmans at Galvin's request].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH TH discussing Elshout translation with Pa' Tuloi. 6 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Separate head houses. 1 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Note on Keraman. 3 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Batu Kawi etc. -- Adat siap. 2 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Tree of life in sickness ceremony. 1 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Tecknonyms (Pa Tuloi) 1 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Added note on Belawing. 1 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Predecessor to Bungan Cult and birds

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter of Raymond Rudes to TH. about visit to Long Sa'an

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Terselah lama bangsa Kayan, T.K. Baling . Foolscap-size hardbound notebook, with 80 p. of handwritten notes in Malay. The bulk of it was written by someone else than Baling, but there are a few notes in Baling's handwriting. There is no date, but it was written in or before 1961 , as it seems to have served as a basis. for the Belaga Book (see below) . Pp. 27-28 have been torn out, and a note on p. 26 says "Start from p. 29"; p. 1-26 have been glued together; there is a partial overlap between the two parts. The various sections are numbered in Malay rather than ciphers, i.e. "bagian ke-dua", "bagian ke-tiga", etc . These have been replaced by ciphers below for convenience .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With a note in TH's hand , " Preliminary". This will be referred tohere as BA/111961A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: BA/111961 With a note in TH's hand , "Out of date" . This will be referred to here as BA/1119618 .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: BA/3/1961 With a note in TH's hand, "Out of date."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TK Baleng, Kayan , B. Sandin. With a note in TH's hand, "Preliminary analysis of ... 20/10/61".This will be referred to as [BN5/1961].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: BELAGABOOK - See Journal for titles. The following description of the Belaga Book indicates between brackets for each section the relevant handwritten documents ; in some cases, more than one version is available. The section numbers have been added by me.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adat adat bangsa Kayan. By Baling. Typewritten in Kayan by the author. 61 p. n.d., apparently writtel). in 1961. The first page (not paginated, and two pages numbered "24", are in Malay). Pp. 1-23 deal with the traditional Kayan religion, pp. 24-61 with the Bungan religion. I have translated this text into English; I plan to annotate it and publish it with an introduction .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kayan. Bahasa Malay (T.K. Baleng), dated 27 x 61 by TH. 2 p. in Baling's handwriting, in Malay, torn from a large notebook. The text has then been typed (3 p.) The text is closely related to the beginning of Adat adat bangsa Kayan .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TK Baleng. Bungan religion. 28-31110/61. 85 p. (only oddnumbered pages have text). In Sandin's handwriting. Apparently a translation of Part II of Adat adat bangsa Kayan. This seems to indicate that Baling translated his Kayan text into Malay (see previous document), which was then translated into English.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mengtahwi adat Bungan Malan Paselong Luan. Adat baru Kayan. 15/11161 by Baling Abun of Uma Aging aged 50 yrs . 26 p. plus 4 p. at beginning, barred off by author. Handwritten in Malay by the author. Transcribed by J.R.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Partial Bungan. Baleng . Schoolbook handwritten by Baling, 14 p. Cover page written by TH. With partial pagination, 5 to 10.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (1) Partial Bungan. Baleng. 26 p. (paginated 35-47). This is. a continuation of the previous item, also in Baling's handwriting. The pagination suggests that there was another schoolbook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: BELAGA TH. Kayan graded society + Masks etc. 54 p. Schoolbook in TH's handwriting. Section numbers added by me. 1. General: Kayan cf Leppo Tau 2. The Kayan Ayou' (Aiyow) 3. Masks: Hudp' 4. Tuans & these rites 5. Animism manifest 6. Papan Ta'kap 7. Totemism relics 8. Kayan cf Kajang 9. Jar relic & Meja jar [torn off] 10. Lack of space ... [no text] 11. The horn bill & the dance [no text] 12. Kin terminology & name use 13. B1rd Omens [no text] 14. Cave Burial! 15. Leadership weaker than Kenyah 16. Batu Keling 17. Kayan ... 18. Teknonymy 19. Divide 20. Aki Dian 21. Ma Pun 22. 23. Bersuma

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Belaga. Admin I. Handwritten by TH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Belaga. Admin II. Handwritten by TH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sec. Burial I. Handwritten by TH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sec. Burial II. Kelirengs Belaga. Handwritten by TH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General Belagalore survey report. By Benedict Sandin, 25 II 61. 9 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kayan shamanism. TH 2/3/61. Approx. 10-15 p. Handwritten by !H. with typewritten note, apparently by Baling, discussing a papan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aki Lengio, G.O.M. of the Western Kayans. Approx. 30 p. Handwritten by TH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Some geographic factors on Borneo's interior life. Approx. 25 p. Handwritten.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH War debt: help others. 3 p. Handwritten by TH. About a Belaga Ukit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: T.K. Baleng/TH Kayans in Apo Kayan, Belungan and elsewhere. 2 p. by TH. 2 p. by Baling. Handwritten.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Puso's Tang jar, 6 p. Handwritten by TH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ini semua lis nama2 orang yang berkerja S.R.D. menulong perintah, pada 1.6.1945. Written by Baling Avun in 1961 or later.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kapuas/6/1961. 7 p. For some reason this is referred to as Book 5 in TH61-57.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kapuas BK 5/61. 35 p. For some reason this is referred to as Book 6 in TH61-57. On the first page, this book has the following note: "This note book is for the Kayan map drawn by Baling A bun".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kapuas/7/61. Not located, may not exist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kapuas/8/61, Kayan folktales. 51 p. Not typed. The text is Malay. It contains three stories: 1. Cherita lima orang adek beradek dengan bantu, pp. 3-23. 2. Cherita Jo Jok Bam, pp. 25-35 3. Hanyang Ketong, pp. 37-51 The informant is Dulah Usat, age 30, Kayan from Uma Suling in Kapuas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kapuas/9/61, Kayan folktales. The text is in Malay. As the previous item, it is in Sandin's handwriting, and is not part of the typed Kapuas book. It contains three stories: 1. [end of story 3 above], p. 3-17. 2. Cherita Lelabi, p. 19-25. 3. Cherita Hila dengan burongjeruit, p. 27-45. The informant for story 2 is Deng Mering, 58 years old, a Kayan from Uma Suling in the Kapuas; Dulah Usat provided the other story. These stories were recorded in Kuching.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcript of Mengie song, Kapuas tape 5. 25 p., handwritten by TH and others. Informant Dullah Uvat [sic]. Despite the absence of corresponding number, constitutes the lOth notebook in the Kapuas 1961 series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: KAPUASBOOK This is a 57 p. typed text in English. It is a transcription ofTH61- 48 to TH61-53. The Kapuas book calls Kapuas/6/1961 "Kapuas book 5", and Kapuas BK 5/61 "Kapuas book 6". The informants were Dian Ndjuk, then 62 years old, Ding Aving, 51 years old, and Dollah Udin, 32 years old, all three Kapuas Kayan. In addition, Baling provided some information. Section numbers have been added by me.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tersebut nama rumah 2 p. Handwritten by Baling

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: KY/1/7 Names of Kayan villages in Kapuas. 2 p. Handwritten

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH with penghule Dian Ndjok, Mah Aging , schoolbook 23 p. Handwritten notes by TH on masks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comment on Mr. Kooijman's draft. 7 p. By TH

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The various peoples of Kalirnantan Barat. by Alexander Kandaran. 4p. "Vari o us·· has been barred and replaced by ""native"' and ··by Alexander Kaodaran·· has been barred. '" BsrrH/63-· ha> been added at the e nd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mukoh t.llamat as related by Pebil Ndju k . 8 VII~- 12 p .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH 15/8164 Ancient scnpt: Mam a t a nd P .T. cf. Chinese Ip

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adat s uen , by Galvin. 7 p .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH 5/12164 Aban Van on Kayau (=Marnat) LongT1kan Kenyah.Sp

scopecontent.p: TH Mamat. 1 p

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discussion with Jalong [about Mamat pictures] , 2 p . And diagram.

scopecontent.p: TH 1118/64 Mamat symbolism. 1 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Rateing [sic] of Mamat grades. Handwritten, 3 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kenyah Mamat. 5 p. Handwritten , n.d .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Mamat. 1 p. Handwritten .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Balan Mukat Langit. 17 p.' Story of Bungan Lesu and Balan Nyaring at Tevau Naa.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH/114/65 Leppo Tau Ken yah masks. 3 p. Also handwritten text.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH 20/1165 Mamat and Turai. 2 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH 6/1165 Mamat. 5 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Megaliths of the Apo Kayan, by Ray Rudes. I. Data Dian burial tomb. II . Data Dian statue of the woman sloping[?] a dog or a hog! III. Long Djeng and Long Lui (Kajan lot) stone stools and 'grinders'. 5 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Mamat paper, addendum. 1 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Note on Mamat. Discussion with Jalong, Leppo Tau of Long Nawang, on basis of his pictures representing sohun grades at mamat. 2 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TH Mamat pictures by Jalong: commentary. 12 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A note on "Laya", by Galvin. 2 p. On the Murik's view of swiftlets. A comment on Galvin [1408].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kenyah family tree by Jalong Liban. 2 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Omen birds, by Jalong Liban. 2 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kenyah marriage customs and adaptations. By Galvin. 8 p., plus Appendix: "Ken yah text", 2 p. With handwritten note, " not. for ~ublication" . Discusses the adaptation of Ken yah marnage ntuals to Roman Catholicism.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Galvin discards for Bilian Siai, n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [Notes by TH on Mamat]. Hard cover notebook. 41 p .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Omen birds, by Jalong Liban, 2 p ., (referr to TH66-6)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oyau Lian Matai ubantolot. 115 p. In Kenyah .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oyau Abeng Lian who died due to sulkiness . 16 p., with sketch map of river Mara. The handwritten ms. is also available, 15 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Teknonyms and burial. 2 p . Handwritten by TH and typed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Apo Kayan and Long Nawang. 2 p. By TH. With handwritten manuscript by TH.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Temenggong Oyong Lawai's genealogy . 1 p. Handwritten

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Brunei Murut origin myth . 29 p.

c02.scopecontent: Scope and ContentsThe last of the Serus. A study in dynami<: decay, by TH, 81p. [incomplete; written after 1949 and before 1966] . I. Background A. The last 500 years II. Extinction-- Submergence III. The last of the Serus IV. The semi and ex-Serus V . Origin and expansion of the Serus VI. Mount Tebujang VII . The downward chain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript on Seru, 110 p.

scopecontent.p: Dayak shield, F. Ling Roth vol 2:129; correspondence with J.F. Johnston, V. of Manchester, Mary Fregear, Ashmolean

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marber Bond (?)
